bestiary.name: Lantern & Blade — Starter Bestiary
bestiary.author: Lantern & Blade Project
bestiary.description: A starter catalog of twelve foes calibrated for low-level play: vermin and small beasts, wasteland humanoids, restless dead, and one true horror. Every entry is self-contained; module authors may add one-off bosses via module_bestiary.

giant_rat.name: Giant Rat
giant_rat.description: A filthy rodent the size of a small dog. Fearless in numbers, cowardly alone, and always, always hungry.

special_abilities.name: Darkvision
special_abilities.description: Sees in total darkness as if it were dim light.

special_abilities.name: Keen Smell
special_abilities.description: Advantage on checks to smell out food, blood, or hidden creatures.

giant_rat.behavior: Swarms from burrows and garbage-heaps. Flees if reduced below half HP unless cornered.
giant_rat.tactics: Runs for ankles and the edges of cloaks; bites, releases, reengages. Rarely fights to the death.

fire_beetle.name: Fire Beetle
fire_beetle.description: A fist-sized iridescent beetle with two soft luminous glands on its thorax. The light is steady and blue-green; the beetles themselves are sluggish but bite surprisingly hard.

special_abilities.name: Illumination
special_abilities.description: The beetle's thorax glands shed dim light in a short radius. The glands continue to glow for 1d6 days after the beetle is killed; dungeoneers harvest them as short-duration lanterns.

special_abilities.name: Darkvision
special_abilities.description: Sees in total darkness as if it were dim light.

fire_beetle.behavior: Docile unless disturbed; defends its nesting chamber to the death.

bandit.name: Bandit
bandit.description: A desperate person with a weapon and a threat. Usually a farmer or a broken soldier. Usually more frightened than they seem.
bandit.behavior: Demands goods before violence. Breaks morale if half the group is down; runs rather than dies for coin.
bandit.tactics: Opens with crossbows from ambush, closes with scimitars if their side has numbers.

cultist.name: Cultist
cultist.description: A hooded figure in a dyed-black robe, face painted with a dull grey sigil. Fervent, under-fed, and harder to talk to than reason suggests.

special_abilities.name: Dark Whisper
special_abilities.description: Once per combat, the cultist may mutter a rote invocation toward a single ally within sight. That ally's next attack before the cultist's next turn has a +2 bonus to hit. Prose-adjudicated; the GM narrates the whisper.

cultist.behavior: Will not parley. Accepts death over capture if the ritual is threatened.

goblin_scout.name: Goblin Scout
goblin_scout.description: A knee-high creature with mottled green skin and eyes too big for its face. Quick, mean, and enormously fond of its own cleverness.

special_abilities.name: Darkvision
special_abilities.description: Sees in total darkness as if it were dim light.

special_abilities.name: Nimble Escape
special_abilities.description: The goblin can disengage or hide as a bonus action on each of its turns. Do not trigger attacks-of-opportunity narration against a goblin scout leaving melee.

goblin_scout.behavior: Never fights fair. Opens with arrows from cover, disengages if pressed, returns when flanked.
goblin_scout.tactics: Keep the scout on the margins of the fight. Concentrate arrows on casters and light-armored targets.

skeleton_warrior.name: Skeleton Warrior
skeleton_warrior.description: A knight's bones lashed back into rough human shape by a cord of grey-black binding. It wears what's left of its old kit — a pitted sword, a scrap of tabard, the memory of a helm. Moves with the patience of something that has forgotten how to be in a hurry.

special_abilities.name: Darkvision
special_abilities.description: Sees in total darkness as if it were dim light.

special_abilities.name: Undying Silence
special_abilities.description: The skeleton makes no sound — no breathing, no grunt of effort, no cry when struck. The only noise it produces is the clatter of its own bones.

skeleton_warrior.behavior: Attacks the nearest living thing without hesitation and without pursuit beyond its bound territory. Falls apart permanently on a killing bludgeon blow.
skeleton_warrior.tactics: Favor hammers, maces, and quarterstaves. Edge weapons chip off chips; weight breaks it properly.

hobgoblin_legionary.name: Hobgoblin Legionary
hobgoblin_legionary.description: Taller than a goblin, broader in the shoulder, red-skinned and helmeted. A hobgoblin legionary was once a soldier in a real army, and still behaves like one — polished gear, straight posture, an uncomfortable amount of discipline.

special_abilities.name: Darkvision
special_abilities.description: Sees in total darkness as if it were dim light.

special_abilities.name: Martial Advantage
special_abilities.description: Once per turn, the legionary deals an extra 2d6 damage on a successful attack against a target that has at least one of the legionary's allies within reach and not incapacitated.

hobgoblin_legionary.behavior: Fights in formation. Holds a line rather than chasing stragglers. Respects a surrender offered in good faith.
hobgoblin_legionary.tactics: Pair legionaries with any ally for the Martial Advantage trigger. Longbow from range when no ally is in melee.

goblin_brute.name: Goblin Brute
goblin_brute.description: A goblin who has grown — half a head taller than its fellows, broader, uglier, with a permanent squint and a warhammer it cannot really afford to lose. The others defer to it, mostly.

special_abilities.name: Darkvision
special_abilities.description: Sees in total darkness as if it were dim light.

special_abilities.name: Savage Rush
special_abilities.description: Once per combat, the brute may charge a target it can see within a short dash. The charge grants advantage on the attack roll and, on a hit, knocks a medium or smaller target prone.

goblin_brute.behavior: Leads from the front. Breaks morale only if its smaller followers have already run.
goblin_brute.tactics: Opens with Savage Rush on whoever looks most dangerous; stays in melee after.

giant_spider.name: Giant Spider
giant_spider.description: A spider the size of a large dog, black-bodied with pale grey leg-joints. It moves with a horrid economy — no step is wasted. Where giant spiders live, their silk always gets there first.

special_abilities.name: Darkvision
special_abilities.description: Sees in total darkness as if it were dim light.

special_abilities.name: Spider Climb
special_abilities.description: Climbs any surface, including ceilings, as easily as a human walks on a floor.

special_abilities.name: Venomous Bite
special_abilities.description: On a successful bite, the target must make a Constitution save (DC 11) or take an additional 2d8 poison damage (half on a successful save) and become poisoned until the end of their next turn.

special_abilities.name: Web
special_abilities.description: Ranged attack, short range. On a hit, target is restrained by sticky webbing. Target may escape with a successful Strength (Athletics) check (DC 12) on their turn; the webbing burns away quickly to fire.

giant_spider.behavior: Ambushes from ceilings and dark corners. Prefers to web a target first, then bite at leisure.
giant_spider.tactics: Lead with Web on a ranged attacker; Bite on whoever's closest next turn.

dire_wolf.name: Dire Wolf
dire_wolf.description: A wolf twice again the size it ought to be, shoulder-height at a grown man's hip. Coarse-furred, yellow-eyed, heavy-jawed. Hunts in pairs and threes.

special_abilities.name: Keen Smell
special_abilities.description: Advantage on Perception checks that rely on smell.

special_abilities.name: Pack Tactics
special_abilities.description: The wolf has advantage on attack rolls against any target that has at least one of its allies within reach and not incapacitated.

special_abilities.name: Trip
special_abilities.description: On a successful bite against a Large or smaller target, the target must make a Strength save (DC 13) or be knocked prone.

dire_wolf.behavior: Works the flanks with a mate or kin. Circles a wounded target rather than committing; commits fully when the target is prone.
dire_wolf.tactics: If the party is a group, separate one and keep them prone. Solo players: favor the bite-then-back-off pattern, trust Pack Tactics to do the math when an ally engages.

restless_spirit.name: Restless Spirit
restless_spirit.description: A half-seen human figure in the shape of whoever it was before — stooped, hooded, walking a loop it cannot finish. The edges of the shape smoke. The cold around it is the specific cold of a room that should not be this cold.

special_abilities.name: Incorporeal Movement
special_abilities.description: The spirit can move through walls, doors, and other solid objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 1d10 force damage if it ends its turn inside a solid object.

special_abilities.name: Darkvision
special_abilities.description: Sees in total darkness as if it were dim light.

special_abilities.name: Bound to Purpose
special_abilities.description: The spirit cannot leave the room or small area that anchors it. If driven out, it vanishes and reforms at the anchor at the next nightfall — unless its unfinished task is completed, in which case it does not return.

restless_spirit.behavior: Acts out the last minutes of its death, over and over. Attacks anything that enters its anchor area once triggered.
restless_spirit.tactics: Resistant to non-magical weapons — the party should lean on magical damage (Lanternblade, wand, spells). A clever party may resolve the spirit's unfinished task instead of fighting.

wight.name: Wight
wight.description: A body preserved past its proper end — skin drawn tight over bone, eyes the color and clarity of frozen water, a voice that still has the tone of whoever it used to be. It remembers things. It has preferences. It is worse than a mindless undead in every way that matters.

special_abilities.name: Darkvision
special_abilities.description: Sees in total darkness as if it were dim light.

special_abilities.name: Life Drain Rider
special_abilities.description: On a successful Life Drain attack, the target must make a Constitution save (DC 13) or have their hit point maximum reduced by the damage dealt. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest; if this reduces a target's maximum to 0, the target dies.

special_abilities.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
special_abilities.description: In direct sunlight, the wight has disadvantage on attack rolls and on Perception checks that rely on sight.

wight.behavior: Taunts. Remembers names. Strikes at whichever target would most hurt the party to lose. Retreats to sacred ground if injured, never surrenders.
wight.tactics: Open with the sword against a tough target to soften them, then switch to Life Drain on the most wounded. Reserve Life Drain for a late-fight killing blow — max-HP reductions matter much more when the fight's decided.
